Title: Engineering biosynthesis of the anticancer alkaloid noscapine in yeast

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potential anticancer drug; noscapinoid precursor platform
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in engineered yeast strains during galactose-based production assays
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous co-expression with plant P450s in yeast enabling production of pathway intermediates detected by LC/MS and NMR
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports activity of multiple plant cytochrome P450 enzymes required for noscapine-pathway reconstitution in yeast
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Galactose carbon source; 25 or 30 °C; feeding with canadine or norlaudanosoline
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as heterologous redox partner from Arabidopsis rather than native poppy CPR; necessary auxiliary factor rather than pathway catalyst
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtATR1 functioned with diverse plant P450s in yeast. 2. Its co-expression enabled CYP82Y1, CYP82X1 and CYP82X2 activities during pathway reconstruction. 3. It was part of strains producing noscapine from canadine or norlaudanosoline.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful enabling component for microbial engineering of plant P450-rich alkaloid pathways

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Protoberberine alkaloid to phthalideisoquinoline alkaloid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates canadine on the nitrogen to form N-methylcanadine
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms pathway intermediate en route to noscapine
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in engineered yeast strains
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stepwise pathway reconstitution in yeast with LC/MS detection of downstream products; NMR verification of intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables conversion of canadine into downstream noscapine intermediates by supplying N-methylcanadine substrate for CYP82Y1
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Galactose medium; fed canadine or norlaudanosoline; 25 or 30 °C
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included among the 16 heterologous plant enzymes for full pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsTNMT functions upstream of CYP82Y1 in yeast. 2. It supplies N-methylcanadine for subsequent hydroxylation. 3. It is required for full noscapine biosynthesis from canadine or norlaudanosoline.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for microbial reconstruction of benzylisoquinoline alkaloid N-methylation steps

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms early committed intermediate toward noscapine
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Bottleneck step during yeast reconstruction before optimization
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologous expression in yeast; highest activity with MSH N-terminal swap and strong GPD or HXT7 promoters at 25 °C
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter and N-terminal engineering in yeast; LC/MS metabolite profiling; NMR verification of product 4
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyses production of 1-hydroxy-N-methylcanadine and strongly affects flux through the reconstituted noscapine pathway
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 25 °C improved activity over 30 °C; galactose medium; fed canadine
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MSH N-terminus-swapped CYP82Y1A improved activity; also produced off-pathway 1-hydroxycanadine from canadine in absence of TNMT
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP82Y1 converts N-methylcanadine to 1-hydroxy-N-methylcanadine in yeast. 2. This step was initially inefficient and required optimization. 3. Promoter engineering, lower temperature and N-terminal swapping increased product titre to ~96.5 μM.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Multiple heterologous P450s lowered pathway efficiency in extended strains
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Major engineering target for improving early oxidation flux in microbial noscapine production

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor for acetylation and secoberberine formation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Bottleneck enzyme; increased copy number substantially raised noscapine titre
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest activity in yeast from HXT7 promoter at 25 °C; additional gene copies further increased activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast pathway reconstitution; promoter and copy-number optimization; LC/MS and NMR verification of product 5
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyses conversion of 4 to 5 and is a major control point for increasing noscapine output
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 25 °C; galactose medium; fed canadine or norlaudanosoline
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Increasing CYP82X2 expression increased extracellular noscapine more than sevenfold from canadine-fed strains and was one of two bottlenecks in norlaudanosoline-fed strains
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP82X2 acts after CYP82Y1 to form 5. 2. HXT7-driven expression and extra copy number markedly increased 5 accumulation. 3. Elevated CYP82X2 expression strongly boosted final noscapine production.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: P450 burden may reduce overall efficiency in strains with many heterologous enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Prime target for pathway balancing and copy-number engineering in microbial production

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acetylates the 13-hydroxyl group of 5 to form 6
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate substrate for CYP82X1-mediated scaffold rearrangement
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in optimized 5-producing yeast strain, often from GPD promoter
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast co-expression with upstream genes; LC/MS and NMR verification of product 6
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Specifically acetylates 5 to generate 6, a required substrate for efficient CYP82X1 function in noscapine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 25 °C; galactose medium; fed canadine or norlaudanosoline
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acetylation was highly specific toward 5; 6 was the only detectable acetylated product
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsAT1 converts 5 to 6 in yeast. 2. The acetylation step is highly specific and precedes efficient CYP82X1 activity. 3. This ordering supports updated pathway sequence.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Necessary module for reconstituting secoberberine branch and noscapine production

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyses protoberberine-to-secoberberine scaffold conversion of 6 to 7; also shows substrate promiscuity on 4 to form 5'
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms direct secoberberine precursor for final conversion steps toward noscapine
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not formally established; promoter-sensitive step for 7 production
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest 5' production under PGK1 promoter; highest 7 production with weaker/late promoters including ADH1, CYC1, HXT7 at 25 °C
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stepwise yeast reconstitution; promoter optimization; LC/MS; NMR verification of 5' and 7
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Performs key scaffold rearrangement to secoberberines, enabling downstream conversion to narcotoline and noscapine
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NMR explicitly established 7 structure; enzyme likely acts efficiently only after PsAT1 acetylation; spontaneous cyclization from 7 to 8 also observed
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP82X1 converts 6 to secoberberine 7, a pivotal scaffold switch in the pathway. 2. It also promiscuously converts 4 to 5'. 3. Yeast-derived products enabled structural validation of secoberberines by NMR.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Promoter optimization differed for native versus off-pathway activities
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable for producing rare secoberberine scaffolds and for flux redirection to novel alkaloids

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Facilitates conversion of 7 to the m/z 402 intermediate consistent with narcotolinehemiacetal (8), likely enhancing cyclization/deacetylation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor for PsSDR1-mediated narcotoline synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Less efficient than PsSDR1; contributes to but is not sole determinant of 9 formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in 7-producing yeast strains under strong promoters
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast expression in 7-producing strains; LC/MS detection of 8 and effect on 9 production with/without PsCXE1
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances conversion of 7 to 8 and promotes downstream narcotoline production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: 8 could not be purified for NMR; data suggest 7 cyclizes spontaneously and PsCXE1 increases this rate; low efficiency limited accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsCXE1 promotes formation of the 8 intermediate from 7. 2. Removal of PsCXE1 strongly decreased but did not abolish 9 synthesis, indicating spontaneous background cyclization. 3. The enzyme likely acts on late secoberberine intermediates before final methylation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Low activity; incomplete conversion may limit flux
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Late-pathway tuning target to improve flux from secoberberine intermediates to narcotoline/noscapine

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Terminal or penultimate step depending on route
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes/dehydrogenates the 8 intermediate to form narcotoline
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; more efficient than PsCXE1 in late-step flux
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Enriched in laticifers according to discussion
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in engineered yeast strains producing 8 or 7-derived intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast co-expression with PsCXE1 and upstream enzymes; LC/MS and NMR verification of narcotoline production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Efficiently converts 8 to narcotoline and drives flux toward the final methylation step
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 25 °C; galactose medium; fed canadine or norlaudanosoline
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors propose PsSDR1 and PsMT2/PsMT3 are likely final two steps in yeast; plant ordering may differ
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsSDR1 efficiently converts 8 to narcotoline in yeast. 2. Its activity is stronger than PsCXE1, increasing late-pathway flux. 3. It likely catalyses one of the final two steps of noscapine biosynthesis in yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key late-pathway enzyme for maximizing narcotoline supply before final methylation

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates scoulerine at the 9-O position in the pathway to canadine
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for downstream noscapine pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Bottleneck in full pathway from norlaudanosoline
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in de novo/extended yeast strains synthesizing noscapine from norlaudanosoline
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Extended pathway assembly from norlaudanosoline; pathway optimization by increasing PsS9OMT expression improved noscapine titre
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Boosts flux from norlaudanosoline toward canadine and final noscapine production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Together with CYP82X2, identified as one of two bottleneck points in the full pathway from norlaudanosoline
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsMT1 functions as the upstream S9OMT in extended noscapine pathway reconstruction. 2. Increased PsS9OMT expression raised noscapine production from norlaudanosoline. 3. It is a key bottleneck in the longer pathway context.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important engineering target for improving upstream precursor flux in full microbial noscapine pathways

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Noscapine biosynthesis / upstream benzylisoquinoline alkaloid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Upstream precursor supply and alternative final methylation complex
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Norcoclaurine-related upstream substrate; narcotoline when in heterodimer with PsMT2
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Upstream methylated BIA intermediate; noscapine in heterodimer context with PsMT2
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions in upstream BIA methylation and can substitute for PsMT3 with PsMT2 to methylate narcotoline to noscapine in yeast
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies upstream precursor flux and can directly form target product via heterodimeric terminal methylation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in full-pathway yeast strains and tested with PsMT2 in 9-producing strains
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast co-expression and in vitro purification of PsMT2/Ps6OMT heterodimer; LC/MS noscapine detection
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as an alternative partner with PsMT2 for final narcotoline 4'-O-methylation and also supports upstream pathway from norlaudanosoline
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 25 °C; galactose medium; fed norlaudanosoline or narcotoline-producing context
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Activity on narcotoline was unexpected and unique to Papaver somniferum 6OMT; Thalictrum flavum 6OMT could not substitute
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ps6OMT can replace PsMT3 as a PsMT2 partner to produce noscapine in yeast. 2. This final-step activity is unique to the poppy enzyme variant. 3. Ps6OMT also served in the extended pathway from norlaudanosoline.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In vitro activity with PsMT2 was lower than PsMT2/PsMT3 despite similar yeast titres
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative enzyme for pathway design and modular final-step methylation engineering

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Upstream benzylisoquinoline alkaloid pathway feeding noscapine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates upstream benzylisoquinoline intermediate during precursor formation toward canadine/noscapine
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in extended pathway strains from norlaudanosoline
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Inclusion in full pathway strains that produced noscapine from norlaudanosoline
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to upstream precursor biosynthesis required for noscapine formation from norlaudanosoline
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used only in the extended pathway from norlaudanosoline; not focal in the stepwise canadine-fed reconstitution
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ps4'OMT was included in the upstream precursor module enabling noscapine synthesis from norlaudanosoline. 2. It supports conversion of early benzylisoquinoline intermediates toward canadine. 3. Its individual contribution was not separately dissected in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream methylation module for complete microbial BIA-to-noscapine pathway

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Upstream benzylisoquinoline alkaloid pathway feeding noscapine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: N-methylates upstream benzylisoquinoline intermediate during precursor formation toward canadine/noscapine
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in extended pathway strains from norlaudanosoline
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Inclusion in multi-enzyme yeast strains that produced noscapine from norlaudanosoline
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports upstream precursor conversion toward noscapine from norlaudanosoline
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used in full pathway assembly, not individually validated here
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsCNMT was part of the precursor module used to synthesize noscapine from norlaudanosoline. 2. It contributes an upstream N-methylation step needed for precursor progression. 3. Its individual effect was not separately quantified in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required upstream methyltransferase for complete de novo-style pathway reconstruction

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Upstream benzylisoquinoline alkaloid pathway feeding noscapine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Forms the berberine bridge during upstream conversion toward scoulerine/canadine precursors
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in extended pathway strains from norlaudanosoline
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Inclusion in extended multi-enzyme reconstitution producing noscapine from norlaudanosoline
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides upstream bridge-forming step necessary to channel precursor flux toward canadine and noscapine
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not individually dissected in current paper; borrowed from prior canadine pathway engineering
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsBBE was included in the upstream module enabling noscapine synthesis from norlaudanosoline. 2. It contributes bridge formation en route to scoulerine/canadine precursors. 3. Its role here is as necessary precursor-supply enzyme in the combined pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core upstream enzyme for full biosynthetic reconstruction from simple BIA precursors

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyses methylenedioxy bridge formation to produce canadine from upstream scoulerine-derived intermediate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies direct precursor for downstream noscapine pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in extended yeast strains synthesizing noscapine from norlaudanosoline
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Inclusion in full pathway strains producing noscapine from norlaudanosoline
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces canadine precursor required to connect upstream BIA pathway to downstream noscapine gene cluster steps
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Chosen from Coptis japonica rather than poppy for the extended pathway assembly
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CjCAS linked the upstream precursor pathway to the canadine-fed noscapine module. 2. Its presence enabled noscapine synthesis from norlaudanosoline. 3. It functions with AtATR1 in yeast as part of the extended route.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Additional P450 load may reduce conversion efficiency in long pathways
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful heterologous CAS for modular assembly of full microbial noscapine pathways

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: O-methyltransferase functioning as heterodimeric terminal pathway regulator/enzyme complex component
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Terminal pathway methylation via heterodimeric enzyme complex
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Narcotoline (substrate) in complex with PsMT3 or Ps6OMT
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms functional heterodimer with PsMT3; also can pair with Ps6OMT
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in narcotoline-producing yeast strains; alone inactive on narcotoline
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast co-expression assays; in vitro enzyme assays with purified proteins; SDS-PAGE, western blot and SEC confirming heterodimer
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required as one subunit of the functional narcotoline 4'-O-methyltransferase complex producing noscapine
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential component of the unique heterodimer that catalyses the final methylation to noscapine
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previous studies suggested PsMT2 acted as S9OMT; current work shows no solo activity on narcotoline and supports heterodimeric terminal methylation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsMT2 alone did not convert narcotoline to noscapine in yeast or in vitro. 2. Co-expression with PsMT3 enabled efficient noscapine formation. 3. Purified PsMT2/PsMT3 complex directly methylated narcotoline, revealing a native-like heterodimeric O-methyltransferase.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Function depends on partner enzyme; standalone annotation can be misleading
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key final-step engineering component; should be deployed with PsMT3 or compatible partner rather than alone

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: O-methyltransferase functioning as heterodimeric terminal pathway regulator/enzyme complex component
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Terminal pathway methylation via heterodimeric enzyme complex
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in 9-producing yeast strains; alone inactive on narcotoline
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast co-expression; in vitro assays with purified heterodimer; western blot and SEC characterization
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts with PsMT2 to convert narcotoline to noscapine; alone cannot catalyse the final step
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Provides the partner subunit needed to create the active final-step O-methyltransferase
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: High sequence similarity to Ps6OMT; evidence supports heterodimer-specific activity absent from homodimer
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsMT3 alone did not synthesize noscapine from narcotoline. 2. Co-expression with PsMT2 generated efficient noscapine production in yeast. 3. Purified PsMT2/PsMT3 heterodimer directly catalysed the final methylation step.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Partner-dependent function complicates annotation and engineering
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Should be co-deployed with PsMT2 for efficient terminal methylation in engineered production systems
